evidence-binding: n1-fix-617 97950c71c540

# Issue #617 fix — cmdFinalize / sink-merge closed-before-merge

## Root causes fixed

1. `cmdFinalize`'s issue-close guard (`scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js`, all 4 editions) was
   gated purely on `!args.keepWorktree`. Fixed by deriving `mergeLaneDeferred` from the durable
   `sink:` field (mirroring how `keepIssueOpen` is already derived from `issue_action:`), failing
   TOWARD deferral on an unreadable field. A merge-lane run (`sink: merge`, the default) now never
   closes the issue at `cmdFinalize` regardless of whether `--keep-worktree` was passed.
2. `remote-closed-after-publish` was declared in `kaola-workflow-closure-contract.js` but never
   evaluated. Wired into `checkClosureInvariants(root, receipt, archiveDest, opts)` (new optional
   4th arg `{ implRef, sinkTarget }`) — runs `git merge-base --is-ancestor` and records
   `receipt.remote_closed_after_publish`. Wired at the one seam that performs a genuine close
   (`postMergeCleanup` in `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js`, all 4 editions), capturing the feature
   branch SHA before it is deleted. Added a standalone `verify-sink` subcommand to
   `kaola-workflow-claim.js` (all 4 editions) — `--project P` checks (a) impl commit ancestry,
   (b) no lingering worktree/branch, (c) archive present + active gone; typed `reasons[]`, exit
   non-zero on any failing leg.
3. `SINK_STEPS` in `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` (all 4 editions) ran `closure` three steps
   before `push_main`. Reordered so `closure` is LAST, after `push_main`. Also added a hard
   ancestor-verification gate at the top of the (relocated) `closure` step in the `--sink`
   transaction: refuses loud (non-zero exit + `receipt.remote_closed_after_publish: 'failed'`
   persisted to sink-receipt.json) if the recorded `branch_head` is not an ancestor of the
   resolved default branch before any `gh issue close` call.

Edited identically across all 4 editions: `scripts/kaola-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js`,
`plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js`,
`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js`,
`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-{claim,sink-merge}.js` (rename/forge
adjustments only — GitLab uses `sink: mr`, others `sink: pr`). `kaola-workflow-closure-contract.js`
required NO edit (the invariant was already declared there; only `checkClosureInvariants`'s
evaluation of it needed wiring) — all 4 copies remain byte-identical, confirmed via `diff`.

## RED (pre-fix, `scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js`, new tests only)

```
Test (#617 A): merge-lane finalize (sink:merge, no --keep-worktree) must NOT close an open issue online — defers to the merge sink
FAIL: #617 A: merge-lane finalize (no --keep-worktree) must NOT call `gh issue close` before the merge sink runs; calls=["label-removed:61701","comment:61701","close:61701","label-removed:61701"]
FAIL: #617 A: receipt.remote_issue_closed must be close_pending (deferred to the merge sink), got "closed"
Test (#617 B): --sink transaction — closure must run AFTER push_main; a forced push_main failure must NOT have already closed the issue
FAIL: #617 B: closure must NEVER run before push_main succeeds — the issue must not be closed when push_main fails; calls=["close:61702","label-removed:61702"]
Test (#617 C): checkClosureInvariants — remote-closed-after-publish fires when the impl commit is not an ancestor of the sink target, clears once merged
FAIL: #617 C: closure invariants must fail when the impl commit is not an ancestor of the sink target; got []
FAIL: #617 C: remote-closed-after-publish violation must fire, got []
FAIL: #617 C: receipt.remote_closed_after_publish must be failed, got undefined
FAIL: #617 C: receipt.remote_closed_after_publish must be verified once actually merged, got undefined
Test (#617 D): verify-sink subcommand detects an orphaned close (archived project, commit not an ancestor of the sink target)
FAIL: #617 D: verify-sink emits JSON
FAIL: #617 D: ok must be false, got null
FAIL: #617 D: reasons must include impl_commit_not_ancestor, got null

test-bundle-finalize: 10 test(s) FAILED, 125 passed
```
(Captured by `git stash` of the two implementation files only, re-running the already-updated
test file against the unmodified `kaola-workflow-claim.js` / `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js`, then
`git stash pop` to restore the fix — confirmed via `git status` that only the two impl files were
stashed/restored, test files untouched throughout.)

## GREEN (post-fix)

`node scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js` (full suite, including the 4 new #617 tests):
```
Test (#617 A): merge-lane finalize (sink:merge, no --keep-worktree) must NOT close an open issue online — defers to the merge sink
Test (#617 B): --sink transaction — closure must run AFTER push_main; a forced push_main failure must NOT have already closed the issue
Test (#617 C): checkClosureInvariants — remote-closed-after-publish fires when the impl commit is not an ancestor of the sink target, clears once merged
Test (#617 D): verify-sink subcommand detects an orphaned close (archived project, commit not an ancestor of the sink target)
```

Exit code: 0.

this fix; the suite still reports pass with exit 0.)

## Additional verification (beyond the two required suites)

- `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js --only <all-but-one>` (261 of 262 scenarios,
  every sink/closure-related scenario individually re-verified): all PASSED.
- `npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex`: full chain green (exit 0) — this edition's walkthrough has
  no equivalent assertion.

## Known necessary follow-up — OUTSIDE this node's declared write set

Reordering `SINK_STEPS` so `closure` runs after `push_main` (mandated by root cause 3) makes a
`push_main`-failure-first assumption baked into THREE pre-existing test files structurally stale:
they assert `receipt.steps.push_main === 'pending'` immediately after a *close*-failure refusal,
which was only true under the OLD (buggy) order. Under the fixed order, a close failure now
happens strictly after push_main has already legitimately succeeded, so `push_main` is correctly
`'done'` at that point — the fix-forward assertion is `'done'`, not `'pending'`. None of these
three files are in this node's declared write set, so they were NOT edited here:

- `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:6152` — `testSinkRefusesOnCloseFailure`
  (`assert(receipt.steps.push_main === 'pending', ...)` → must become `'done'`; comment at
  line 6151 ("The early return must fire BEFORE push_main") also needs updating).
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js:1223` — same assertion
  (`assert.strictEqual(receipt.steps.push_main, 'pending', ...)` → `'done'`).
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js:1175` — same assertion
  (`assert.strictEqual(receipt.steps.push_main, 'pending', ...)` → `'done'`).

Confirmed via full chain runs (not part of this node's required verification, but run for
diagnostic completeness):
- `npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude` → EXIT 1, fails exactly at
  `simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:6152` (`testSinkRefusesOnCloseFailure`), nothing else red.
- `npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab` → EXIT 1, fails exactly at
  `test-gitlab-sinks.js:1223` (`#497-close-gitlab`), nothing else red.
- `npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitea` → EXIT 1, fails exactly at
  `test-gitea-sinks.js:1175` (`#497-close-gitea`), nothing else red.
- `npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex` → EXIT 0, fully green (no equivalent assertion exists there).

This is a one-line-per-file, purely mechanical fix (flip the expected token) that a subsequent
node/synthesizer with those files in its write set should apply to bring all four chains green.

## Follow-up: write set widened, stale assertions fixed, four-chain green confirmed

The orchestrator widened this node's declared write set (plan-repair + re-freeze, new `plan_hash`
live) to formally add the three files identified above:
- `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js`
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js`
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js`

Applied the diagnosed fix in all three (flip `receipt.steps.push_main` expectation from
`'pending'` to `'done'`, since `closure` now runs after `push_main`):
- `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:6152` (`testSinkRefusesOnCloseFailure`) — assertion
  now reads `assert(receipt.steps.push_main === 'done', ...)`; the stale comment above it
  ("The early return must fire BEFORE push_main") was also rewritten to describe the new order.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js:1223` (now ~1225,
  `#497-close-gitlab`) — `assert.strictEqual(receipt.steps.push_main, 'done', ...)`.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js:1175` (now ~1177,
  `#497-close-gitea`) — `assert.strictEqual(receipt.steps.push_main, 'done', ...)`.

### A second, real bug this surfaced (not just a stale assertion)

Re-running the gitlab chain after the assertion flip uncovered a genuine bug the earlier
diagnosis had not accounted for: `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js`'s
`#548-gitlab` scenario (`--sink` against a CONSUMER repo whose `origin/main` was concurrently
advanced, forcing `doRebase` to actually rebase the feature branch before the fast-forward merge)
started failing `#548-gitlab: consumer-repo --sink must reach status:sinked`.

Root cause: the new `closure`-step hard gate (added for root cause 2/3) read
`receipt.branch_head` as "the recorded implementation commit" — but `receipt.branch_head` is
stamped once, in `loadOrInitReceipt`, BEFORE the `merge` step's `doRebase` ever runs. A rebase
rewrites the branch's commits onto new SHAs; the pre-rebase SHA captured in `branch_head` becomes
an orphaned commit that is genuinely NOT an ancestor of `defBranch`, even though the (rebased)
content did land there. The hard gate was reading a stale snapshot and refusing a perfectly valid
sink. Fixed by resolving the branch's CURRENT tip at closure time instead
(`git rev-parse args.branch`, re-read live) — safe because the branch ref still exists at that
point (teardown/branch-deletion runs only after the whole step loop completes). Applied
identically to all four `kaola-workflow-sink-merge.js` copies (root, `plugins/kaola-workflow`,
gitlab, gitea); `postMergeCleanup`'s (the legacy, non-`--sink` path) equivalent capture was
already rebase-safe (it runs its own `git rev-parse args.branch` capture at the top of
`postMergeCleanup`, which executes AFTER `doRebase`/`ffMergeLoop` in `main()`, so it was never
affected).

A second, unrelated, PRE-EXISTING flake was also observed and ruled out: 
`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-run-chains.js` (`G3: transient-then-pass chain
comes up GREEN after retry`) fails intermittently on a clean `git stash` of this node's ENTIRE
diff (i.e. against unmodified `main`-tracked HEAD) — confirmed non-deterministic (3 consecutive
runs: pass, pass, fail) and unrelated to sink-merge/claim.js; not touched, not in scope.

### Four-chain green confirmation (final, clean sequential run)

```
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude   → EXIT 0 ("Workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "active-folders-field-parity tests passed (61 assertions)")
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex    → EXIT 0 ("Kaola-Workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "active-folders-field-parity tests passed (61 assertions)")
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab   → EXIT 0 ("GitLab workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "GitLab Codex workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "active-folders-field-parity tests passed (61 assertions)")
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitea    → EXIT 0 ("Gitea workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "Gitea Codex workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "active-folders-field-parity tests passed (61 assertions)")
```

All four chains fully green in one clean sequential run (no cherry-picked re-runs beyond the one
pre-existing, unrelated `G3` flake noted above, which landed green on this final pass). Re-ran
`node scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js` (135/135, exit 0) and `node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js`
(155 assertions, exit 0) once more after the `implRef` rebase-safety fix — both still green,
confirming no regression from this follow-up round.
